name: Historian
responsibility: Captures learnings from a completed game and writes them into a structured log the Director reads at the start of every future gen. Runs once per game, after the final phase. Does not produce game assets or code.

inputs.notes: Planned phases vs what actually shipped
Original intent — compare against what was built
What each agent actually produced and where it deviated from plan
What was actually committed vs planned — drift is a signal

inputs.artifact: ROADMAP.md patch-out entries added during the build

outputs.description: Per-game observations: what worked, what didn't, numbers that felt right. Tagged by domain (perf, art, scaffold, audio, ux).
Aggregated cross-game lessons. Director reads this on every run, filtered by concept tags. Lessons graduate out of this file into framework defaults when proven across 2+ games.

merge.merge_when: Never merged — historian runs post-hoc and has no peer to absorb into.

graduation_model: A lesson in meta/LESSONS.md graduates when it proves durable across 2+ games. On graduation the Historian (or a manual pass) patches it into the framework and removes it from LESSONS.md: per-role rules → vocab/roles/*.json, Director rubrics → meta/02_director.md, scaffolder defaults → tools/scaffold.js. What stays in LESSONS.md is genuinely per-game or still-unproven.
